decision: Create a portable Spec Agent with specification and evolution guidance.
follow_ups: Forward-test on a realistic specification update.
rationale: Establish a portable baseline for spec-driven product decisions.
spec_delta: Added the initial Spec Agent, evolution, and system behaviors.
title: Spec Agent Bootstrap
user_intent: Create a Spec Agent package for spec-driven development, evolving specifications, drift tracking, and a future timeline UI.

assumptions: Structural drift is automatable; semantic drift requires product review.
decision: Adopt Provenance-style behavior backlinks for drift observation.
follow_ups: Verify the end-to-end flow in a real target repository.
rationale: Stable behavior identity enables stale-rule detection without making code authoritative.
spec_delta: Added product behavior for phantom, dead, silent, unlinked, and stale-verification findings.
title: Backlink Drift Checker
user_intent: Use Provenance-style backlinks to detect stale specs and code, reconcile drift, and capture the result in evolution history.

assumptions: Historical Provenance mentions remain context, but runtime guidance must be standalone.
decision: Remove the external Provenance CLI as a runtime requirement.
rationale: The portable package must work without an unavailable external command.
spec_delta: Clarified the portable checker as the active drift mechanism.
title: Remove Prov CLI Assumption
user_intent: Make the imported Spec Agent folder work without requiring the Provenance CLI.

assumptions: A concise existing index is preferable to another navigation document.
decision: Strengthen the existing specification index with routing and recovery guidance.
spec_delta: Added centralized task routing to the existing index.
title: Add Routing Index
user_intent: Give agents one map showing which package files are related and what to read for each task.

assumptions: Installer CLI and production timeline UI remain outside this architecture phase.
decision: Approve canonical skill sources with framework discovery paths.
follow_ups: Adopt the approved architecture in the normative specifications.
rationale: Canonical sources reduce duplication while preserving agent discovery.
spec_delta: Approved the production skill architecture for normative adoption.
title: Approve Production Skill Architecture
user_intent: Adopt production skill discovery conventions while preserving spec-driven workflow, evolution, backlinks, and drift.

assumptions: Existing history and normative specifications must be migrated rather than discarded.
decision: Use canonical skill sources with deterministic agent discovery.
follow_ups: A future installer may copy discovery assets into target repositories.
rationale: Production-style discovery should not create competing normative sources.
spec_delta: Revised product and system contracts for the focused-skill architecture.
title: Refactor To Provenance Skill Layout
user_intent: Refactor Spec Agent to Provenance-style discovery and make evolution, backlinks, and drift first-class skills.

assumptions: Three focused skills provide the smallest clear ownership model.
decision: Consolidate to spec-request-flow, spec-drift-sync, and spec-evolution under one skill root.
rationale: Three independent triggers create clear ownership without parallel structures.
spec_delta: Adopted the consolidated Spec Agent contract.
title: Consolidate To One Skill Root
user_intent: Use only .agents/skills, eliminate duplicate sources, and preserve spec-driven development, evolution, timelines, drift, and traceability.

assumptions: Root SPEC.md remains the predictable entrypoint and each feature receives a bounded packet.
rationale: Bounded packets preserve discovery while limiting artifact growth.
spec_delta: Added FSP-001 through FSP-006 and indexed the feature packet.
title: Feature Specification Packets
user_intent: Create at most two specification files per feature without losing requirements, approval, drift, evolution, or timeline behavior.

decision: Use one append-only events.jsonl as the product-history source and generate Markdown/Mermaid views.
rationale: One structured stream avoids per-event file growth and supports UI consumers.
spec_delta: Added ELOG-001 through ELOG-006 and product-history ownership.
title: Consolidate Evolution Into JSONL
user_intent: Replace accumulating per-event Markdown files with one structured evolution log while preserving history, UI data, and generated diagrams.

assumptions: Three focused skills remain independently discoverable.
decision: Approve standalone specification gates with progressive disclosure.
rationale: Reliable spec-driven behavior must not depend on optional external skills.
spec_delta: Approved PSH-001 through PSH-012 for gated request flow, authority-aware drift, and boundary-based evolution.
title: Approve Production Skill Hardening Specification
user_intent: Review the three Spec Agent skills against Superpowers brainstorming and official Agent Skills guidance, then harden them for reliable spec-driven development.

assumptions: The accepted product contract replaces the earlier mixed lifecycle.
decision: Adopt the approved skill behavior as the current product contract.
rationale: The approved skill behavior became current product truth.
spec_delta: Accepted PSH-001 through PSH-012 and updated the root contract.
title: Adopt Production Skill Hardening
user_intent: Adopt the approved production hardening across the three Spec Agent skills and supporting specification workflow assets.

decision: Use a strict Spec Agent that outputs product-only spec.md and acceptance.md, emits the exact Code Agent handoff, and delegates all planning and implementation.
rationale: Separating product authority from engineering execution satisfies the audited Spec/Plan boundary while derived traceability preserves drift visibility.
spec_delta: Replaced plan.md with acceptance.md, removed implementation orchestration and drift repair, isolated code links in derived traceability, and narrowed evolution to product decisions.
title: Strict Spec Agent Separation
user_intent: Refactor Spec Agent to stop after approved product specifications while preserving two spec files, Code Agent discovery, read-only drift, traceability, and evolution history.

assumptions: Python packaging is the approved first distribution; npm can be considered later.
decision: Distribute the strict Spec Agent as spec-agent-cli with an explicit, safe, idempotent spec-agent init command.
follow_ups: Create the GitHub repository, configure the PyPI pending trusted publisher, and publish the first GitHub release.
rationale: The Provenance installation pattern makes the skills portable while keeping repository changes explicit and preserving one authored skill source.
spec_delta: Added CLI distribution rules, acceptance outcomes, safe initialization behavior, and trusted publishing requirements.
title: CLI Distribution Approved
user_intent: Package Spec Agent like Provenance so anyone can install the CLI and explicitly initialize a repository.

assumptions: Linked-file fingerprints are conservative structural evidence; semantic authority remains human.
decision: Make backlinks, traceability refresh, and clean validation mandatory in the Code Agent handoff while keeping product specifications implementation-independent.
rationale: Drift detection requires durable implementation evidence connected to stable product behavior IDs.
spec_delta: Added the Code Agent traceability contract, schema-v2 backlink baselines, changed-code findings, approved-only validation, and CLI validation commands.
title: Enforce Code and Specification Traceability
user_intent: Require every Code Agent implementation to preserve specification-to-code backlinks so future agents can detect stale specifications and code drift.
